releases.title: collectZ v3.4.121
releases.summary: This release adds a vertical poster-only layout option for Plex Now Playing displays. It keeps the existing saved display preferences and display-token flow intact while giving passive screens a clean full-height poster mode.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.120
releases.summary: This release adds saved Plex Now Playing display preferences. Admins can tune the passive display surface for `/now-playing` and display-token links without changing Plex imports, webhooks, scheduled sync, or watch-state behavior.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.119
releases.summary: This release adds a revocable Plex Now Playing display link. Admins can generate a limited token for `/now-playing?token=...` so a passive display can show the sanitized Plex Now Playing viewer without an admin browser session.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.118
releases.summary: This release adds a standalone Plex Now Playing page. Authenticated admins can open `/now-playing` for a passive display view backed by the saved Plex now-playing readback and an app-owned image proxy.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added the `/now-playing` frontend route outside the dashboard shell.
Added a read-only backend viewer endpoint for sanitized active-session display data.
Added an authenticated Plex image proxy for Plex-relative poster/art keys.
Shows current title, show/season context, player state/platform, progress, and poster/art when Plex exposes usable image keys.
Added empty and unavailable states for no active sessions or unreachable Plex settings.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.117
releases.summary: This release adds a real Plex PMS now-playing proof path. collectZ can now use saved Plex settings from the running stack to probe `/status/sessions` and write secret-free evidence about what a future Now Playing viewer can rely on.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a backend proof script for saved real Plex now-playing readback.
Captured viewer-readiness field coverage for active sessions, including progress, player state, Plex-relative poster/art key availability, and queue-presence hints.
Extended sanitized now-playing session readback with Plex-relative `metadataKey`, `thumbKey`, `artKey`, and `hasQueueItem` fields.
Kept the proof read-only and separate from imports, webhooks, scheduled sync, watch-state writes, and the dedicated viewer.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.116
releases.summary: This release adds a read-only Plex now-playing view inside Integrations. The Plex settings panel can now call the saved now-playing readback endpoint and show sanitized active-session rows without adding webhooks, scheduled sync, watch-state writes, or import changes.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added an `Active Sessions` action to the Plex Integrations panel.
Displayed sanitized Plex session rows with title, type, progress, user label, and player state/platform.
Added an explicit `No active Plex sessions.` empty state after checking now-playing readback.
Reused the same platform/workspace endpoint-base wiring as the existing Plex diagnostics.
Preserved existing Plex provider discovery, section discovery, imports, and now-playing endpoint behavior.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.115
releases.summary: This release exposes the proven Plex now-playing parser through read-only admin and workspace integration endpoints. collectZ can now call saved Plex settings for `/status/sessions` and return sanitized active-session readback without adding UI, webhooks, sync behavior, or import changes.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added admin and workspace Plex now-playing readback endpoints.
Reused saved Plex API URL and token settings server-side.
Returned only sanitized session fields such as title, type, progress, user label, and player state.
Added a Docker-runnable fake PMS smoke that exercises the collectZ endpoint path.
Preserved existing Plex provider discovery, section discovery, and import behavior.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.114
releases.summary: This release adds a read-only Plex now-playing provider proof. collectZ can now parse the PMS `/status/sessions` shape into a safe internal readback contract, backed by a fake-PMS runtime smoke, before any Now Playing UI, webhook, sync, or import behavior is added.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added Plex `/status/sessions` to the provider-oriented PMS modernization contract.
Added safe JSON/XML parsing for now-playing session payloads.
Normalized only non-sensitive readback fields such as title, type, progress, user label, and player state.
Added a Docker-runnable fake PMS smoke that writes secret-free now-playing evidence.
Preserved existing Plex provider discovery, section discovery, and import behavior.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.113
releases.summary: This release adds a read-only Plex provider discovery probe for saved Plex integrations. Admin and workspace integration screens can now ask the configured Plex Media Server for `/media/providers` and show sanitized capability readback without exposing tokens or changing Plex import behavior.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added admin and workspace Plex provider discovery probe endpoints.
Reused saved Plex API URL and token settings server-side.
Returned only sanitized provider fields: key, title, type, protocol, identifier, and feature keys.
Added a Plex Integrations action to probe providers and display the sanitized readback.
Preserved existing Plex section test and import behavior.

releases.title: collectZ v3.4.112
releases.summary: This release proves the new Plex `/media/providers` discovery path against a PMS-shaped runtime response without changing the existing Plex import flow. The smoke starts a fake Plex Media Server, calls the real provider-discovery fetch path, and writes secret-free evidence showing the normalized provider readback.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a backend Plex provider-discovery smoke that exercises `fetchPlexMediaProviders()` against a fake PMS server.
Wrote provider-discovery evidence to `artifacts/plex-provider-discovery/plex-provider-discovery-smoke.json`.
Verified the provider readback only exposes safe fields: provider key, title, type, protocol, identifier, and feature keys.
Kept Plex tokens, provider URLs, file paths, and raw locations out of smoke output and browser-visible contracts.
Preserved the current legacy Plex import paths; this release does not rewrite imports.
